item.title: Analysis of the exam system at University F**
item.description:
Source: Facebook

This is an analytical post regarding the USB-based final exam system used by University F**. This is Windows To Go - a complete Windows OS contained within a 64GB USB drive, encrypted with Bitlocker.

Read full: here.

Quick Summary:

How it works

  • Students plug the USB into their personal laptops and boot from the USB instead of using the pre-installed operating system.

item.title: Jper - Japanese Kana Games
item.description:
Feeling nervous about the various Japanese alphabets and the thought of memorizing them all?

Don’t worry! Jper: Japanese Kana Games is here to transform your learning journey from a daunting task into an exciting and effortless experience.

Whether you are a complete beginner or a kid starting to explore a new language, Jper provides the perfect foundation for mastering the Japanese alphabet through listening and play.

🎮 LEARN THROUGH PLAY

Say goodbye to boring rote memorization! Jper uses audio-based interactive gameplay to help you master both Hiragana and Katakana. By matching sounds to characters, your brain retains the alphabet faster and more naturally.

item.title: Getting Started with Linux
item.description: This page is temporarily unavailable due to remastering.

item.title: Linux for Beginner
item.description:
If you’re new to Linux, this guide will help you get started with one of the most powerful and versatile operating systems out there. Linux runs everything from smartphones to supercomputers, and it’s known for its stability, security, and flexibility.

In this post, we’ll explore what Linux is, how it works, and introduce you to popular beginner-friendly distributions like Ubuntu or Linux Mint. You’ll also learn the basics of navigating Linux, both through its graphical interface and the command line, so you can confidently take your first steps into the Linux world.

item.title: Why Lua is good for beginners?
item.description:
Hello there! This is my first blog post about programming languages. Today’s topic is why you should learn Lua in this year and why it’s an excellent choice for beginners.

Introduction

If you haven’t heard of Lua, it’s a really small programming language. Maybe you have heard of it, but never had a reason to learn it because your preferred programming language already meets all your needs.

Lua is an open source language built on top of the C programming language. It is exclusively designed for embedded systems. It features dynamic typing, simple syntax and efficient memory management.

item.title: About
item.description:
Who is Kiyoshi Datto?

Hi there! I’m Kiyoshi Datto (aka Ha Nguyen Tien Dat or DatIT), currently a Software Engineering student. I love exploring new technologies in this rapidly evolving tech era. The programming languages I have learned and often (or not) use for projects include: Java, JavaScript, Python và Golang.

Why this page exists?

This website serves as an archive for all my projects, ranging from university assignments to personal projects. Additionally, the Blog section is where I share technical knowledge and coding experiences I’ve gained, and it is not intended for teaching purposes.

item.title: Privacy Policy and Personal Data Protection
item.description:
FireHelper regards your privacy as a very serious matter. FireHelper is committed to protecting users’ privacy, and this privacy policy explains our collection, use, and disclosure of data. This personal data protection policy applies to all websites we support, mobile applications, and all our other service platforms.

Through this policy, users will clearly understand how FireHelper processes and protects users’ Personal Data during the process of searching, accessing, registering accounts, and using FireHelper’s services; ensuring that users’ Personal Data is always private and secure in accordance with legal regulations and our security standards.

item.title: Introducing UrGift App
item.description:
[⚠ EOL] This project was retired on February 19th, 2026.

The new way to express your feelings to the one you love.

Confessing love with technology is a new and modern way compared to traditional methods. UrGift is a fun app that helps you express your feelings in a unique and exciting way. Based on the original confession website for Desktop, UrGift Android has a simple and optimized design. Some features from the Desktop version have been removed, but new and interesting ones have been added to make your confession even better.

item.title: Setup and Fix Windows Tool (SAFW)
item.description:
[⚠ NOTE] This project will be retired on December 31st 2026.

A streamlined command-line utility for quick access to Windows settings and troubleshooting common issues. Key features include:

  • System Configuration: Access Computer Management, System Properties, and Programs.

  • Network & Devices: Manage Network, Sharing, and Print options.

  • Customization: Modify Personalization and Power settings.

  • Troubleshooting Tools: Fix Windows Updates, Wi-Fi issues, and view activation status.

  • Utilities: Date/Time settings, Region, System Info, Activate Windows and more.

item.title: Lumia 520: My First Smartphone
item.description: Microsoft officially pulled the plug on Windows Phone on January 14, 2020, right as the legendary Windows 7 was being phased out to make way for a new generation. Back then, Lumia devices were custom-built by Nokia and Microsoft specifically to run on this platform. For me, the Lumia 520 wasn’t just a popular budget phone, it was my very first smartphone, the one that introduced me to a whole new world of technology. Fast forward to 2024, with AI dominating every conversation, I suddenly felt a wave of nostalgia. Since my old 520 had long since given up the ghost, I decided to pick up a Lumia 640 XL to see how this “relic” holds up in today’s digital world.

item.title: Portfolio Windows 95 Website Project Completed ✔
item.description:
Bringing the classic Windows 95 experience to the modern browser. This project is a fun experiment with Web Components to create a creative and interactive portfolio. Hope you have a blast exploring it!

View online demo here.

Changelog here.

item.title: ReSolve
item.description:
[⚠ EOL] This project was retired on August 30th 2023.

🔎 Operations:

August 29, 2023: Support for all project-related information will cease.

August 30, 2023: All access and download links for the ReSolve program project will be removed.

⚠ NOTICE: Users have to uninstall all Resolve tool files (if any are present on their devices).

We will not be responsible for any issues or damages that may arise from the program after August 29, 2023.

item.title: Catch Me If You Can
item.description:
This is an official version of my first awesome game. The game is very easy to play, simply follow the instructions. Hopefully, you’ll enjoy it.

Try it directly here or you can downloads it:

Version Name Download Size Release Date

item.title: Lemon App
item.description:
[⚠ EOL] This project was retired on December 31st 2020.

Dear player, we have discontinued support for the Lemon App after December 31, 2020, in order to focus on creating a more exciting product that brings greater benefits to users. We sincerely thank you for trusting and using our product.

Info

There is no information for this App.

Android 4.0+ or higher required

Download responsibly!

Download

item.title: Restore Wifi (Fix Wifi Tool)
item.description:
[⚠ EOL] This project was retired on December 15th 2020.

Hello, we may have some unfortunate news for you. The Restore Wifi (Fix Wifi) tools will officially be discontinued on December 15, 2020.

After this date, there will be no new updates or patches available for this tools. Therefore, you have to uninstall the tool because hackers may attack and steal your data.

Frequently Asked Questions

Question: Will the tool still work if it is retired?
Answer: The tool would still work if it were retired.

item.title: GSC - Google Slides Crawler
item.description:
Want to download a Google Presentation for offline viewing, but the owner has disabled the download button? Don’t worry! Google Slides Crawler (GSC) is here to save the day!

Google Slides Crawler (GSC) is an open-source browser extension designed for Chromium-based browsers (such as Chrome, Edge, Brave…).

Instead of manually taking screenshots of every slide, GSC automates the process of crawling data from the Google Slides display interface, then reconstructs and exports it into a high-quality PDF format. It is the perfect solution for archiving study materials from courses or webinars where the owner has restricted direct download permissions.

item.title: Talking About MacBook and MacOS: Why I Hate It
item.description:
Let’s go back to 2001, when Apple introduced MacOS with big promises of a shiny interface that was “so easy that anyone could use it.” Then in 2006, the MacBook arrived, and everyone thought it would rule the laptop world. But reality hit differently. After spending some time with it, I eventually had to say goodbye for a few reasons: 👇

I have to admit, MacOS on a MacBook is undeniably smooth and well-optimized. However, there are certain things that make the experience quite… frustrating. This post is just my personal take on why this duo failed to meet my expectations, and why you shouldn’t treat them like some kind of “holy grail” of technology.
